methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: update_market checks whitelist and updates accCantoPerShare
transformations: int256 _delta cast to uint256 for balance adjustment
data_flows.trust_changes: Lending market is fully trusted to report correct delta values. No verification of actual cNOTE balances.
validation_points: Solidity 0.8 automatic underflow check on user.amount -= uint256(-_delta)
exposure_risks: Compromised or malicious whitelisted lending market can fabricate arbitrary deltas for any lender address
No cross-reference with actual on-chain token balance of the lending market
manipulation_risks: A whitelisted lending market can inflate user.amount without actual deposits, enabling fraudulent reward claims
Repeated tiny deposits can exploit rounding truncation in rewardDebt calculation

path: Each iteration: compute epoch, blockDelta, cantoReward via gauge weight
transformations: epoch = (i / BLOCK_EPOCH) * BLOCK_EPOCH (floor to epoch boundary)
nextEpoch = i + BLOCK_EPOCH (BUG: should be epoch + BLOCK_EPOCH)
data_flows.trust_changes: External dependency on GaugeController.gauge_relative_weight_write for weight data; return value directly used in reward calculation
validation_points: Whitelist check at start of update_market
marketSupply > 0 check to avoid division by zero
exposure_risks: accCantoPerShare and secRewardsPerShare are public and can be read by anyone
manipulation_risks: CRITICAL: nextEpoch = i + BLOCK_EPOCH incorrectly skips epoch boundaries causing wrong reward rates applied to wrong blocks
GaugeController weight manipulation could influence reward distribution
accCantoPerShare cast to uint128 may silently truncate or revert causing permanent DoS

path: update_market called to bring accCantoPerShare current
user.rewardDebt = accumulatedCanto (state updated before external call)
Native CANTO sent via msg.sender.call{value: cantoToSend}()
transformations: uint256 multiplication then int256 cast for accumulatedCanto
Subtraction of signed integers for cantoToSend
int256 to uint256 cast for msg.sender.call value
data_flows.trust_changes: State is updated before external call (CEI pattern), but no reentrancy guard exists
exposure_risks: No reentrancy guard; if msg.sender is also a whitelisted market, cross-function reentrancy via sync_ledger possible during callback
manipulation_risks: Reward calculation based on buggy accCantoPerShare may over- or under-pay users
Contract may have insufficient CANTO balance to pay claims (no balance check before send)

path: setRewards sets cantoPerBlock for epoch range
whiteListLendingMarket toggles whitelist status and initializes lastRewardBlock
transformations: lastRewardBlock set to current block.number on whitelist
data_flows.trust_changes: Governance has unilateral control with no timelock or multisig requirement
exposure_risks: Governance can set arbitrary reward rates, potentially draining contract CANTO
manipulation_risks: setRewards can retroactively change reward rates for past epochs if update_market hasnt been called yet
De-whitelisting a market locks unclaimed user rewards permanently
setRewards loop has no gas limit — large epoch ranges can cause out-of-gas

core_rules: Rewards distributed proportionally to user share of market total balance
Reward accumulation follows MasterChef accRewardPerShare pattern
Only whitelisted lending markets can trigger balance updates via sync_ledger
Rewards sourced from native CANTO held by the contract
Epoch-based reward rate configuration with BLOCK_EPOCH = 100,000 blocks
GaugeController weights determine per-market reward allocation
economic_incentives: Users incentivized to deposit cNOTE into lending markets for CANTO rewards
Gauge weight voting determines which markets receive more rewards
Secondary reward tracking (secRewardsPerShare) for external integrations
governance_mechanisms: Single governance address with no timelock or multisig requirement
Governance sets reward rates per epoch via setRewards
Governance controls market whitelist via whiteListLendingMarket
Governance can transfer its own role via setGovernance
No proposal, voting, or delay mechanism — instant execution

edge_cases.scenario: update_market called when lastRewardBlock is not epoch-aligned (which is the common case)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Loop should correctly split reward calculation at epoch boundaries, applying the correct cantoPerBlock rate for each epoch
edge_cases.actual_behavior: nextEpoch = i + BLOCK_EPOCH instead of epoch + BLOCK_EPOCH causes the loop to skip actual epoch boundaries. Rewards are calculated using the wrong epoch's cantoPerBlock for blocks that span multiple epochs. This systematically misallocates rewards.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker can time update_market calls to benefit from using a higher-rate epoch's cantoPerBlock for blocks that should use a lower rate, or vice versa. The effect depends on the direction of rate changes across epochs. Can be gamed by strategically triggering updates.

edge_cases.scenario: Zero market supply during active reward period
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Rewards for zero-supply blocks should be deferred or redistributed
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Rewards for zero-supply blocks are permanently lost — the loop skips reward accumulation when marketSupply == 0 but advances lastRewardBlock past those blocks
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A user could withdraw all funds temporarily (making supply 0), causing some reward blocks to be skipped, then re-deposit to capture subsequent rewards — effectively wasting protocol funds

edge_cases.scenario: accCantoPerShare or secRewardsPerShare exceeds uint128 max
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should handle large accumulated values gracefully
edge_cases.actual_behavior: uint128() cast will revert in Solidity 0.8.x for values exceeding uint128.max, permanently bricking update_market and all dependent functions (sync_ledger, claim) for that market
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If market supply is very small (e.g., 1 wei) while rewards are high, accCantoPerShare grows extremely fast. An attacker could deposit dust to inflate accCantoPerShare and permanently DoS a market.

edge_cases.scenario: Repeated dust deposits to exploit rewardDebt rounding
edge_cases.expected_behavior: rewardDebt should accurately track entitled rewards regardless of deposit size
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each deposit computes rewardDebt += (delta * accCantoPerShare) / 1e18 with truncating division. Many small deposits truncate more total debt than one large deposit, giving the attacker slightly more claimable rewards
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Rounding dust attack — profit per transaction is tiny (< 1 wei per deposit), but with gas-free or very cheap transactions could be marginally profitable

edge_cases.scenario: Market de-whitelisted while users have unclaimed rewards
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Users should be able to claim accumulated rewards before de-whitelisting takes effect
edge_cases.actual_behavior: After de-whitelisting, update_market reverts with 'Market not whitelisted', blocking both claim() and sync_ledger(). All unclaimed rewards are permanently locked.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Governance (or compromised governance) can de-whitelist a market to prevent users from claiming legitimately earned rewards. No grace period or migration path.

edge_cases.scenario: Governance retroactively changes reward rates for past epochs
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Past epoch rewards should be immutable once set
edge_cases.actual_behavior: setRewards can overwrite cantoPerBlock for any epoch. If update_market hasn't been called for a market since those epochs, the new rate applies retroactively to that market's reward calculation.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Governance can inflate or deflate past rewards for markets that haven't been updated recently, creating unfair reward distribution

edge_cases.scenario: int256 overflow when computing accumulatedCanto in claim()
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should handle large reward values without overflow
edge_cases.actual_behavior: int256(uint256(user.amount) * market.accCantoPerShare / 1e18) reverts if the uint256 result exceeds type(int256).max. This would permanently prevent claiming for affected users.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: With very large user.amount or very large accCantoPerShare, the claim function becomes permanently non-functional for that user in that market

edge_cases.scenario: claim() comment says 'only for prior epochs' but code allows current epoch claims
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Per NatSpec, rewards should only be claimable for finished epochs
edge_cases.actual_behavior: No check prevents claiming rewards accumulated in the current, unfinished epoch. Users can claim immediately as rewards accrue block-by-block.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Design intent mismatch. If the intent was to restrict to past epochs, current implementation allows early claiming which could enable front-running of gauge weight changes.

edge_cases.scenario: First deposit to a freshly whitelisted market
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Rewards should start accumulating from the first deposit
edge_cases.actual_behavior: lastRewardBlock is set to block.number at whitelist time. If no deposits occur for many blocks, those reward blocks are skipped (marketSupply = 0). This is correct but means configured rewards for those blocks are wasted.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not directly exploitable but leads to reward wastage if markets are whitelisted well before first deposit

assumption_violations: ASSUMPTION: nextEpoch = i + BLOCK_EPOCH correctly advances to the next epoch boundary. VIOLATION: This is only correct when i is epoch-aligned. When i is mid-epoch (common case), it overshoots the next boundary, causing wrong epoch rate to be applied to a range of blocks.
ASSUMPTION: Lending markets always honestly report deposit/withdrawal deltas. VIOLATION: A compromised or malicious whitelisted market can fabricate arbitrary deltas for any user address.
ASSUMPTION: Contract always has sufficient CANTO balance to pay claims. VIOLATION: No check that contract balance >= cantoToSend before transfer attempt.
ASSUMPTION: accCantoPerShare fits in uint128 indefinitely. VIOLATION: With low market supply and high reward rates, this can overflow, bricking the market.
ASSUMPTION: gauge_relative_weight_write is a pure view-like function. VIOLATION: The _write suffix implies state mutation, which could have side effects during update_market.
game_theory_risks: Strategic update_market timing: Users can choose when to trigger update_market to benefit from the epoch boundary bug, applying a favorable epoch rate to more blocks
Deposit/withdraw sandwich: A user can deposit right before a high-reward epoch and withdraw right after, concentrating rewards while minimizing exposure time
Market supply manipulation: In low-liquidity markets, a whale can withdraw to zero supply (wasting rewards) then re-deposit to capture subsequent rewards at favorable accCantoPerShare
Gauge weight gaming: If gauge weights change between epochs, users can time updates to lock in favorable weights
integration_risks: GaugeController dependency: gauge_relative_weight_write is called within a loop that could execute many iterations. If GaugeController is slow or reverts, update_market becomes DoS'd.
Lending market trust: The protocol delegates all balance accounting to external lending markets. A compromised market breaks the entire reward system for all its users.
Native CANTO transfers: msg.sender.call{value}('') can fail for contracts without receive/fallback, locking user rewards
Secondary reward tracking (secRewardsPerShare) is purely internal accounting with TODO comment — no clear integration spec for third-party consumers
calculation_errors: CRITICAL: update_market line 'uint256 nextEpoch = i + BLOCK_EPOCH' should be 'uint256 nextEpoch = epoch + BLOCK_EPOCH'. Current code fails to split reward calculation at actual epoch boundaries, applying incorrect cantoPerBlock rates to blocks spanning multiple epochs.
Truncation in rewardDebt calculation during sync_ledger: (delta * accCantoPerShare) / 1e18 truncates, allowing marginal rounding profit via many small deposits.
secRewardsPerShare calculation has TODO comment and no proper scaling factor — likely produces incorrect secondary reward values for tokens with different decimals.

token_semantics.notes: Native gas token. Sent via low-level call{value}. No ERC20 interface. Contract receives via receive() payable.

Not directly transferred by this contract. Balance changes tracked indirectly through sync_ledger delta reports from lending markets. No SafeERC20 interaction needed as the contract never touches cNOTE directly.

invariants.name: user_balance_sum_equals_market_total
invariants.notes: For each market, the sum of all user amounts must equal the total market balance. Maintained by sync_ledger but depends on honest lending market reporting.

invariants.name: reward_debt_consistency
invariants.notes: At any sync point, rewardDebt should equal the integral of (amount * accCantoPerShare_at_deposit_time / 1e18). Rounding truncation causes small systematic drift favoring depositors.

invariants.name: contract_solvency
invariants.notes: Contract native CANTO balance must be >= sum of all pending claimable rewards. NOT enforced anywhere — governance must manually ensure sufficient funding.

invariants.name: accCantoPerShare_monotonically_increasing
invariants.notes: accCantoPerShare only ever increases (+=). Ensures cantoToSend in claim() is non-negative for users who haven't withdrawn.
